c74object.name: dk.descriptorframe
c74object.digest: Analyzes audio for several audio descriptors based on frame input
c74object.description: The dk.descriptorframe object outputs loudness, centroid, spectral flatness, and pitch along with the derivatives for loudness/centroid/flatness and confidence for pitch.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithms have a look at these articles:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/loudness
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/spectralshape

inlet.description: Frame to analyze from dk.onsetframe~, dk.realtimeframe~, or dk.triggerframe~

attribute.name: blocking
attribute.digest: Choose which thread to use for processing
attribute.description:
By default non-realtime objects use the Max main thread.
The available options are:
0: Non-blocking = Use a dedicated worker thread (for longer jobs)
1: Blocking (Low Priority) = Use Max main thread (for small jobs)
2: Blocking (High Priority) = Use calling thread (for very small jobs)

attribute.name: fftsettings
attribute.description:
FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:
For the hop size, -1 = windowsize/2
For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
Constraints
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size

attribute.name: inputfilter
attribute.description:
Messages related to descriptor filtering.
After sending the filter message you send a series of additional arguments that determine what descriptor is being filtered, how it is being filtered, as well as further filtering strands combined with conditionals.
The available descriptors that can be used for filtering are:
loudness = loudness in dB
loudness_derivative = derivative of loudness in dB
centroid = centroid in MIDI pitch
centroid_derivative = derivative of centroid in MIDI pitch
flatness = spectral flatness in dB
flatness_derivative = derivative of spectral flatness in dB
pitch = pitch in MIDI pitch
pitch_confidence = pitch confidence in 0 to 1
You can filter by the following operators:
== = equals
!= = does not equal
< = less than
<= = less than or equal
> = greater than
>= = greater than or equal
You can join multiple queries using:
and
or
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
inputfilter loudness < -45 or inputfilter centroid > 60 and flatness < -80

attribute.name: numframes
attribute.description: How many samples to analyse. The default of -1 indicates to analyse through to the end of the buffer~.

attribute.name: realtime
attribute.digest: Runs in high-priority thread for realtime performance
attribute.description: Runs in high-priority thread when fast realtime performance is required.

attribute.name: select
attribute.digest: Select a subset of descriptors to pass through
attribute.description: Allow only a subset of the descriptors to come out of the list output (buffer~ output is unaffected).

attribute.name: default
attribute.value: loudness, loudness_derivative, centroid, centroid_derivative, flatness, flatness_derivative, pitch, pitch_confidence

attribute.name: source
attribute.digest: The buffer to use as source material
attribute.description: The buffer~ to use as the source material to be pitch-tracked. The different channels of multichannel buffers will be processed sequentially.
